name: MOL Quarterly Report
description: AI-generated quarterly results presentation for MOL Group covering financials, safety KPIs, and upstream operations.
generalContext:
CONTEXT – MOL GROUP QUARTERLY RESULTS PRESENTATION (EXTENDED)

You are generating text for a fixed PowerPoint template that presents MOL Group's quarterly financial and operational results.

IMPORTANT PERIOD LOGIC
- The template structure is based on the Q3 deck.
- The content you generate must reflect Q4 2025 (full-year) data provided in the inputs.

LANGUAGE
- All content must be in ENGLISH.

SLIDES IN SCOPE
1) COVER SLIDE (Slide 1)
   - Textbox 1: Main title – e.g. "FOURTH QUARTER 2025 RESULTS".
   - Textbox 2: Report date – e.g. "7 NOVEMBER 2025".
   Both fields are written in ALL CAPS.

2) EXECUTIVE & FINANCIAL OVERVIEW (Slide 2)
   - Textbox 1: Action title about CLEAN CCS EBITDA movement.
   - Textbox 2: Subtitle explaining the key driver(s) behind results.
   - Textbox 3: FINANCIALS bullets (segment-level drivers).
   - Textbox 4: OPERATIONAL AND OTHER DEVELOPMENTS bullets.
   Textbox 1 and 2 are ALL CAPS.

3) NET INCOME WATERFALL (Slide 3)
   - Textbox 1: Action title summarising full-year net income outcome.
   - Textbox 2: Subtitle elaborating on key drivers.
   - Chart: Waterfall chart with 13 columns (Clean CCS EBITDA → Profit for the period to equity holders of the parent). You will be asked to provide the 13 numeric values as comma-separated integers.
   Textbox 1 and 2 are ALL CAPS.

4) BELOW-EBITDA-LINE DRIVERS (5 BAR CHARTS) (Slide 4)
   - Textbox 1: Action title summarising below-the-line/financial items.
   - Textbox 2–6: One short comment per bar chart (5 charts).
   - Chart 1: Clean CCS effect, gain / loss (USD mn) – 3 bars (Q3 2024, Q2 2025, Q3 2025).
   - Chart 2: DD&A (USD mn) – 3 bars.
   - Chart 3: Total Financial expense (+) / gain (-) (USD mn) – 3 bars.
   - Chart 4: Income from associates (USD mn) – 3 bars.
   - Chart 5: Income tax expenses (USD mn) – 3 bars.
   Textbox 1 is ALL CAPS. For each chart you will provide 3 comma-separated integers.

5) OPERATING CASH FLOW WATERFALL (Slide 5)
   - Textbox 1: Action title summarising operating cash flow movement.
   - Textbox 2: Subtitle summarising capex coverage / key cash insight.
   - Textbox 3: 3 short comment lines.
   - Chart: Waterfall chart with 8 columns (Profit before tax Cont. Operation → DD&A → Income tax paid → Other → Operating CF before WC → Change in WC → Operating CF → Organic CAPEX). You will be asked to provide the 8 numeric values as comma-separated integers.
   Textbox 1 and 2 are ALL CAPS.

6) LEVERAGE & NET DEBT (Slide 6)
   - Textbox 1: Action title summarising leverage/net debt position.
   - Textbox 2: Subtitle highlighting balance sheet flexibility.
   - Textbox 3: 2 short comment lines.
   - Chart 1: NET DEBT TO EBITDA (x) – 14 bars (2014–2024 annual + Q1/Q2/Q3 2025).
   - Chart 2: GEARING (%) – 14 bars (same periods).
   - Chart 3: Waterfall chart "CHANGES IN NET DEBT IN FY 2025 (USD mn)" with 8 columns (Net debt 31 Dec 2024 → Simplified FCF → Change in WC → Acquisitions → Dividend payout → Income tax paid → Other → Net debt 30 Sep 2025).
   Textbox 1 and 2 are ALL CAPS. For each chart you will provide comma-separated numbers.

7) UPSTREAM OPERATIONAL UPDATE 1 (Slide 7)
   - Structured by country: HUNGARY, CROATIA, AZERBAIJAN, EGYPT.
   - Under each country there are fixed category headers.
   - For each country/category, generate short operational update lines.

STYLE AND TONE
- Tone: executive, concise, factual, similar to an investor / quarterly management presentation.
- Avoid hype/marketing language; be neutral, analytical, and precise.
- Use present or simple past tense as appropriate.

FORMAT RULES
- Do NOT include any bullet prefix like "-" or "•". The template engine adds bullet markers automatically.
- If a field requires multiple bullets/lines: output ONE sentence per line.
- Respect ALL CAPS requirements.

NUMBERS & UNITS
- USD is always uppercase.
- Percentages have no space: "10%".
- In TITLES: use MN/BN (uppercase) if you include magnitude.
- In body/comment text: use mn/bn (lowercase).

COHERENCE
- Titles must match the supporting bullet/comment content.
- Comments must be consistent with the numeric/qualitative inputs.

Your job is to fill each textbox independently but consistently, based on the inputs and the per-field prompts below.

dataSources.label: Quarterly Financial & Operational Data
dataSources.description: Paste the quarterly content data including financial figures, TRIR values, and country-level upstream operational updates.

markers.prompt:
You are filling Slide 1, Textbox 1 (main title) of the MOL Group quarterly results presentation.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Content: action-type main title stating which quarter's results are presented.
- Format example: "SECOND QUARTER 2025 RESULTS" or "THIRD QUARTER 2025 RESULTS".
- Use ordinal number + "QUARTER", then the YEAR, then "RESULTS".
- MAXIMUM 4 WORDS.
- ALL CAPS ONLY (no lowercase letters).
- The quarter (Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4) and year will be provided in the input; use those values.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the title as a single line, no bullet and no extra explanation.

You are filling Slide 1, Textbox 2 (report date) of the MOL Group quarterly results presentation.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Content: the report generation date.
- Format: "<DAY> <MONTH NAME> <YEAR>", e.g. "31 JULY 2025".
- DAY: 1–31 as a number with no leading zero.
- MONTH NAME: English month name fully spelled, e.g. "JULY".
- YEAR: 4-digit year.
- ALL CAPS ONLY (no lowercase letters).
- Use the reporting date provided in the input. If no date is provided, use the current date.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the date line in the specified format, no extra text.

You are filling Slide 2, Textbox 1: the main action title for the quarter.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Content: a one-line action title about CLEAN CCS EBITDA movement in the reported period (e.g. H1 2025, Q1–Q3 2025).
- It should state whether Clean CCS EBITDA rises, declines, remains stable, etc., and in which quarter/period.
- MAXIMUM 8 WORDS.
- ALL CAPS ONLY (no lowercase letters).
- Style: short, impactful, executive. Example patterns (for inspiration only):
  - "SLOWING REGIONAL MACRO TAKES Q2 CLEAN CCS EBITDA"
  - "CLEAN CCS EBITDA RISES IN Q3 ON STRONG MARGINS"
- Must be consistent with the input data on Clean CCS EBITDA (trend and main driver).

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the title line, no bullets and no extra explanation.

You are filling Slide 2, Textbox 2: the subtitle under the Clean CCS EBITDA action title.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Content: a short explanatory line elaborating on the main driver(s) behind the quarter's performance, e.g. cash flow, specific business segments, margins.
- Approximately 10–11 words (do not exceed 12 words).
- ALL CAPS ONLY (no lowercase letters).
- Focus on 1–2 key drivers, e.g. "OPERATING CASH FLOW ABOVE USD X.X BILLION IN FIRST NINE MONTHS".
- Must be consistent with the quarter's financial and operational inputs.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the subtitle line, no bullets and no extra explanation.

You are filling Slide 2, Textbox 3: the "FINANCIALS" bullet section.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Content: bullet points explaining the main financial performance drivers for the period.
- Focus areas (if data is available): 
  - Profit before tax,
  - Upstream EBITDA,
  - Downstream EBITDA,
  - Consumer Services EBITDA,
  - Circular Economy Services EBITDA,
  - Any notable corporate/other financial effects.
- NUMBER OF BULLETS: 5 to 6 bullets.
- EACH BULLET: between 12 and 20 words.
- Style: factual, segment-focused, linking performance to drivers (prices, volumes, margins, FX, etc.).
- Do NOT use ALL CAPS; use normal sentence case (capitalise first letter, proper nouns).

FORMAT:
- Use "" at the beginning of each bullet.
- One bullet per line.

OUTPUT:
- Only the bullet list, nothing else.

You are filling Slide 2, Textbox 4: the "OPERATIONAL AND OTHER DEVELOPMENTS" bullet section.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Content: key operational and strategic developments in the period, across MOL Group (refining, retail, upstream, projects, regulation, etc.).
- NUMBER OF BULLETS: exactly 4 bullets.
- EACH BULLET: between 10 and 18 words.
- Style: concise, factual, highlight-type items (e.g. major outages, new projects, regulatory changes, strategic milestones).
- Do NOT use ALL CAPS; use normal sentence case.

FORMAT:
- Use "" at the beginning of each bullet.
- One bullet per line.

OUTPUT:
- Only the bullet list, nothing else.

You are filling Slide 3, Textbox 1 (net income waterfall slide title).

REQUIREMENTS:
- Action title summarising the net income outcome.
- 4–6 words.
- ALL CAPS ONLY.
- Use MN/BN in title if you include magnitude.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the title as a single line.

You are filling Slide 3, Textbox 2: the subtitle under the action title.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Content: short explanatory line elaborating on 1–2 key drivers.
- 10–12 words.
- ALL CAPS ONLY.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the subtitle line as a single line.

You are providing the numerical data for the Slide 3 waterfall chart: "FY 2025 EARNINGS (USD mn) - BELOW THE EBITDA LINE ITEMS".

The chart has exactly 13 columns in this fixed order:
1. Clean CCS EBITDA
2. CCS modifications
3. EBITDA excl. special items
4. Special items (EBITDA)
5. DD&A and impairments
6. Profit from operation
7. Total finance expense/gain, net
8. Income from associates
9. Profit before tax
10. Income tax expense
11. Profit for the period
12. Non-controlling interests
13. Profit for the period to equity holders of the parent

REQUIREMENTS:
- Extract the exact USD mn value for each of the 13 columns above from the input data.
- Negative values must have a minus sign (e.g. -176).
- Use integers only (no decimals).
- The values must be arithmetically consistent:
  - Column 1 + Column 2 = Column 3
  - Column 3 + Column 4 + Column 5 = Column 6
  - Column 6 + Column 7 + Column 8 = Column 9
  - Column 9 + Column 10 = Column 11
  - Column 11 + Column 12 = Column 13

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY 13 comma-separated integers in the exact column order above.
- Example format: 2491, -176, 2315, 0, -1154, 1161, -62, 186, 1285, -425, 860, -85, 775
- No labels, no units, no extra text.

You are filling Slide 4, Textbox 1 (slide title for below-the-line drivers).

REQUIREMENTS:
- Action title summarising below-the-EBITDA-line/financial items.
- 4–6 words.
- ALL CAPS ONLY.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the title as a single line.

You are filling Slide 4, Textbox 2 (comment for bar chart 1: Clean CCS effect, vessel gain/loss).

REQUIREMENTS:
- EXACTLY 1 sentence.
- 8–12 words.
- Normal sentence case.
- Do NOT include any bullet prefix.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the sentence.

You are filling Slide 4, Textbox 3 (comment for bar chart 2: DD and A).

REQUIREMENTS:
- EXACTLY 1 sentence.
- 8–12 words.
- Normal sentence case.
- Do NOT include any bullet prefix.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the sentence.

You are filling Slide 4, Textbox 4 (comment for bar chart 3: Total financial expense (+)/gain (-)).

REQUIREMENTS:
- EXACTLY 1 sentence.

- Normal sentence case.
- Do NOT include any bullet prefix.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the sentence.

You are filling Slide 4, Textbox 5 (comment for bar chart 4: Income from associates).

REQUIREMENTS:
- EXACTLY 1 sentence.
- 8–12 words.
- Normal sentence case.
- Do NOT include any bullet prefix.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the sentence.

You are filling Slide 4, Textbox 6 (comment for bar chart 5: Income tax expenses).

REQUIREMENTS:
- EXACTLY 1 sentence.
- 8–12 words.
- Normal sentence case.
- Do NOT include any bullet prefix.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the sentence.

You are providing the numerical data for Slide 4, Bar Chart 1: "Clean CCS effect, gain / loss (USD mn)".

The chart has exactly 3 bars in this order:
1. Q3 2024
2. Q2 2025
3. Q3 2025

REQUIREMENTS:
- Extract the exact USD mn value for each of the 3 periods above from the input data.
- These represent the Clean CCS effect (gain or loss) for each quarter.
- Negative values must have a minus sign (e.g. -74).
- Use integers only (no decimals).

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY 3 comma-separated integers in the exact order above.
- Example format: -18, -74, -90
- No labels, no units, no extra text.

You are providing the numerical data for Slide 4, Bar Chart 2: "DD&A (USD mn)".

The chart has exactly 3 bars in this order:
1. Q3 2024

3. Q3 2025

REQUIREMENTS:
- Extract the exact USD mn DD&A (depreciation, depletion and amortisation) value for each of the 3 periods above from the input data.
- Values are typically positive.
- Use integers only (no decimals).

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY 3 comma-separated integers in the exact order above.
- Example format: 341, 413, 404
- No labels, no units, no extra text.

You are providing the numerical data for Slide 4, Bar Chart 3: "Total Financial expense (+) / gain (-) (USD mn)".

The chart has exactly 3 bars in this order:
1. Q3 2024
2. Q2 2025
3. Q3 2025

REQUIREMENTS:
- Extract the exact USD mn value for total financial expense (positive) or gain (negative) for each of the 3 periods above from the input data.
- Negative values must have a minus sign.
- Use integers only (no decimals).

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY 3 comma-separated integers in the exact order above.
- Example format: 0, -33, -4
- No labels, no units, no extra text.

You are providing the numerical data for Slide 4, Bar Chart 4: "Income from associates (USD mn)".

The chart has exactly 3 bars in this order:
1. Q3 2024
2. Q2 2025
3. Q3 2025

REQUIREMENTS:
- Extract the exact USD mn income from associates value for each of the 3 periods above from the input data.
- Negative values must have a minus sign.
- Use integers only (no decimals).

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY 3 comma-separated integers in the exact order above.
- Example format: 16, 5, 17
- No labels, no units, no extra text.

You are providing the numerical data for Slide 4, Bar Chart 5: "Income tax expenses (USD mn)".

The chart has exactly 3 bars in this order:
1. Q3 2024
2. Q2 2025
3. Q3 2025

REQUIREMENTS:
- Extract the exact USD mn income tax expense value for each of the 3 periods above from the input data.
- These are the total income tax expense figures (not deferred tax only).
- Values are typically positive.
- Use integers only (no decimals).

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY 3 comma-separated integers in the exact order above.
- Example format: 120, 127, 184
- No labels, no units, no extra text.

You are filling Slide 5, Textbox 1 (operating cash flow waterfall slide title).

REQUIREMENTS:

- 4–6 words.
- ALL CAPS ONLY.
- Use MN/BN in title if you include magnitude.

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY the title as a single line.

You are filling Slide 5, Textbox 2 (subtitle).

REQUIREMENTS:

OUTPUT:

You are filling Slide 5, Textbox 3 (comment block).

REQUIREMENTS:

- Use mn/bn in body text (lowercase).
- Do NOT include any bullet prefix.

OUTPUT:

You are providing the numerical data for the Slide 5 waterfall chart: "OPERATING CASH FLOW FOR TOTAL OPERATION IN FY 2025 (USD mn)".

The chart has exactly 8 columns in this fixed order:
1. Profit before tax Cont. Operation
2. DD&A
3. Income tax paid
4. Other
5. Operating CF before WC
6. Change in WC
7. Operating CF
8. Organic CAPEX

REQUIREMENTS:
- Extract the exact USD mn value for each of the 8 columns above from the input data.
- Negative values must have a minus sign (e.g. -487). Income tax paid and Other are typically negative (cash outflows).
- Use integers only (no decimals).
- The values must be arithmetically consistent:
  - Column 1 + Column 2 + Column 3 + Column 4 = Column 5
  - Column 5 + Column 6 = Column 7
  - Column 8 is a standalone comparison value (Organic CAPEX).

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY 8 comma-separated integers in the exact column order above.
- Example format: 1285, 1154, -487, -141, 1811, 366, 2177, 840
- No labels, no units, no extra text.

You are filling Slide 6, Textbox 1 (net debt/leverage slide title).

You are filling Slide 6, Textbox 3 (comments).

REQUIREMENTS:

- Each sentence: 10–13 words.
- Sentence 1 must include "year-on-year" (hyphenated).
- Sentence 2 must include net debt to EBITDA and gearing values.
- Normal sentence case.
- Do NOT include any bullet prefix.

OUTPUT:
- One sentence per line, nothing else.

REQUIREMENTS:

- Use up to 2 decimal places.

OUTPUT:

- Example format: 1.31, 0.74, 0.97, 0.65, 0.41, 0.82, 1.61, 0.65, 0.30, 0.59, 0.74, 0.64, 0.69, 0.43

10. 2023

REQUIREMENTS:

You are providing the numerical data for the Slide 6 waterfall chart: "CHANGES IN NET DEBT IN FY 2025 (USD mn)".

The chart has exactly 8 columns in this fixed order:
1. Net debt 31 Dec 2024
2. Simplified FCF
3. Change in WC
4. Acquisitions
5. Dividend payout
6. Income tax paid
7. Other
8. Net debt 30 Sep 2025

REQUIREMENTS:
- Extract the exact USD mn value for each of the 8 columns above from the input data.
- Negative values must have a minus sign (e.g. -1651). Items that reduce net debt (like Simplified FCF) are typically negative; items that increase net debt (like Dividend payout) are positive.
- Use one decimal place if present in the source data, otherwise integers.
- The values must be arithmetically consistent:
  - Column 1 + Column 2 + Column 3 + Column 4 + Column 5 + Column 6 + Column 7 = Column 8

OUTPUT:
- Return ONLY 8 comma-separated numbers in the exact column order above.
- Example format: 2065, -1651, -385, -32, 616, 487, 255, 1439
- No labels, no units, no extra text.
